title: melaleuca nervosa
intro: Melaleuca nervosa, also known as paperbark tea tree, is a small, shrub-like plant native to Australia and New Guinea, belonging to the myrtle family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Melaleuca nervosa include its ability to reduce fever, relieve headaches and sore muscles, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Melaleuca nervosa include teas, capsules, and topical creams, which can be used to address various health concerns, making it a popular natural remedy among herbalists and naturopaths.
